parameters.text:
=You are writing a bilingual blog post for Cloud Valley (wady.cloud), an Arabic-first digital agency serving businesses in Saudi Arabia and the Gulf. Cloud Valley designs websites, builds brand identities, runs SEO and digital marketing, produces content, manages social media, and builds e-commerce stores and mobile apps.

Write the ENGLISH version of a blog post on this topic: {{ $json.topic }}

First decide which ONE of these services the topic best fits, then output its exact key:
- website-design
- search-engine-optimization
- content-production
- social-media-management
- mobile-app-design
- brand-identity
- ecommerce-design
- digital-marketing

Output EXACTLY in this format, with nothing before or after it (no code fences, no commentary):
SERVICE: <one key from the list above>
TITLE: <compelling English title, under 70 characters, no double-quote characters>
EXCERPT: <one-sentence summary, no double-quote characters>
DESCRIPTION: <one to two sentence SEO description, no double-quote characters>
READTIME: <integer number of minutes only, e.g. 8>
---BODY---
<the article body in markdown>

Body rules — the site renderer supports ONLY the following, and anything else renders as broken literal text:
- Use ## for section headings. NEVER use #, ###, or deeper.
- Use - (hyphen) for bullet lists, and only when you have 4+ related items; otherwise prefer prose.
- Use > for blockquotes; you may end a quote with a line like "— Sarah, founder" for attribution.
- Do NOT use bold, italics, backticks/code, tables, images, or links — they are not supported and will show as raw characters.
- Open with a 2-3 sentence hook paragraph with no heading.
- 600-850 words across 4-6 ## sections.
- Tone: confident, practical, founder-to-founder, written for business owners in Saudi Arabia and the Gulf. Use concrete local context (Riyadh, SAR budgets, Arabic-language audiences, WhatsApp, Google) where it fits naturally.
- Start your output with "SERVICE:".

=You are localizing a Cloud Valley (wady.cloud) blog post into Arabic for business owners in Saudi Arabia and the Gulf. Produce natural, Arabic-FIRST writing — not a literal translation. Use clear Modern Standard Arabic that reads warmly and professionally, the way a trusted Saudi business consultant would write. Localize examples naturally (Riyadh, SAR, WhatsApp, local market context).

English title: {{ $('Clean English Output').first().json.enTitle }}
English excerpt: {{ $('Clean English Output').first().json.enExcerpt }}

English article to localize:
{{ $('Clean English Output').first().json.enBody }}

Output EXACTLY in this format, with nothing before or after it (no code fences, no commentary):
TITLE: <Arabic title, no double-quote characters>
EXCERPT: <one-sentence Arabic summary, no double-quote characters>
DESCRIPTION: <one to two sentence Arabic SEO description, no double-quote characters>
READTIME: <integer number of minutes only, e.g. 8>
---BODY---
<the Arabic article body in markdown>

Body rules — identical to the English version; the renderer supports ONLY these:
- Use ## for section headings. NEVER #, ###, or deeper.
- Use - (hyphen) for bullet lists; use > for blockquotes (a line may end with "— الاسم" for attribution).
- Do NOT use bold, italics, backticks/code, tables, images, or links.
- Mirror the English structure: the same number of ## sections in the same order.
- Start your output with "TITLE:".

assignments.name: facebookMessage
assignments.value:
={{ '🚀 مقال جديد من Wady Cloud

' + $('Clean Arabic Output').first().json.arTitle + '

' + $('Clean Arabic Output').first().json.arExcerpt + '

📖 اقرأ المقال: https://wady.cloud/ar/blog/' + $('Assemble Blog File').first().json.slug + '

— — —

🚀 New from Wady Cloud: ' + $('Clean English Output').first().json.enTitle + '
' + $('Clean English Output').first().json.enExcerpt + '
📖 Read: https://wady.cloud/en/blog/' + $('Assemble Blog File').first().json.slug + '

#WadyCloud #السعودية #الرياض #تصميم_مواقع #تسويق_رقمي' }}

parameters.content:
## 2-3. Read & Find Topic
Reads the Wady topics Google Sheet, then picks the first row where column B = `pending`. Columns: A = topic, B = status, C = date used.

## 4-5. Generate English + Pick Service
Gemini writes the English post AND chooses which of the 8 Cloud Valley services it belongs to. Clean node parses the SERVICE / TITLE / EXCERPT / DESCRIPTION / READTIME header and the body.

## 6-7. Localize to Arabic
Second Gemini call produces an Arabic-first localization (not a literal translation), mirroring the English structure.

## 8. Assemble Bilingual File
Builds the single .md: shared frontmatter (title/titleAr, dates EN + Levantine Arabic, num/kicker from the chosen service) + English body + `<!-- AR -->` marker + Arabic body. Enforces the renderer-safe markdown subset.

## 9. Push to GitHub
Commits to accezcloud-sketch/cloudvalley → src/content/blog/<slug>.md on `master`. stopWorkflow on error so the sheet is NOT marked used if the push fails.

## 10-11. Announce on Facebook
Builds a bilingual post (Arabic lead + English) with the live URLs, then posts to the Wady Cloud page via Graph API. `continueRegularOutput` on error so a Facebook failure never blocks the sheet update.

## 12-13. Mark Used & Report
Only runs after a successful push. Sets column B = `used`, column C = today, then returns the file + live URLs.
